toolChanges.reason: Added by init --auto for ECMAScript best practices (AvoidWithStatement, ConsistentReturn, UseBaseWithParseInt, AssignmentInOperand, UnnecessaryBlock, EqualComparison); 0 issues found — confirms good JavaScript fundamentals.

patternChanges.reason: Convention mismatch — the project design system explicitly uses hex colors (#1a1a1a, #ffffff) as documented in CLAUDE.md. 56 of 56 issues were in the design token file (variables.css). Requiring CSS custom properties instead contradicts established project conventions.

Cross-tool deduplication — Stylelint declaration-no-important covers the same !important concern on the identical CSS module files. Stylelint is the more appropriate specialized CSS tool. Keeping both generates duplicate issues on the same lines.

100% false positives in this codebase — all 8 hits are SVG path data strings and CSS attribute selectors (e.g. 'link[rel="canonical"]', 'meta[name="keywords"]') that trigger Biome's entropy heuristic. Security concern remains fully covered by Trivy_secret and Semgrep gitleaks/hard-coded-password patterns.

Convention mismatch for developer documentation — technical terms like HMR, ESLint, Vite, TypeScript are standard vocabulary in a developer-oriented project. Flagging them as undefined acronyms does not add actionable value.
Low signal for technical documentation — pronoun usage in developer agent instructions rarely creates genuine ambiguity in context. 3 issues, all Info severity.
Restored threshold of 12 from previous config (new default is 10); reduces noise on established React SPA while keeping meaningfully complex functions flagged.
Restored threshold of 75 from previous config; appropriate for a React SPA where some components are legitimately longer than the default 50-line threshold.

deduplication: Biome noImportantStyles disabled — same CSS !important concern covered by Stylelint declaration-no-important (more appropriate CSS-specific tool)
Biome noSecrets disabled — 100% false positives (SVG paths, CSS selectors); same concern covered by Trivy_secret and Semgrep hard-coded-password/gitleaks patterns
noisyButKept: Stylelint declaration-no-important (23 issues, Warning) — kept; !important in CSS animation/reset files is a genuine code smell worth tracking even though the project uses it intentionally in places

keyImprovements: Removed 56 false-positive hex color warnings — the project design system intentionally uses hex colors (#1a1a1a, #ffffff) as documented in CLAUDE.md; all hits were in variables.css
Eliminated 29 duplicate !important warnings by deduplicating Biome vs Stylelint coverage on the same CSS module files; Stylelint's 23 findings are retained as the authoritative CSS tool
Disabled Biome noSecrets — all 8 findings are false positives (inline SVG paths and CSS attribute selectors); security concern covered by Trivy_secret and Semgrep credential patterns
Restored 19 Critical-severity Lizard complexity issues across 12 components that were absent from the previous config — these flag genuinely overgrown functions needing refactoring attention

Disabled 19 low-signal Agentlinter informational warnings (undefined technical terms, ambiguous pronouns in dev docs); 22 higher-signal warnings remain including escape-hatch and duplicate-instruction checks
PMD7 added for ECMAScript best practices; 0 findings confirms the codebase avoids with-statements, inconsistent returns, and similar JS pitfalls

Restored 7 patterns dropped by AllCritical→Critical filter regression: Lizard_file-nloc-critical, Lizard_parameter-count-critical, shellcheck_SC1070/SC1073/SC2114, Biome noUndeclaredVariables/noUnresolvedImports — these cover oversized files, destructive shell commands, and JS correctness in non-TypeScript files

localConfigTools.note: ESLint9 runs with the project's own eslint.config.js. Issues from this tool are controlled by that config, not Codacy patterns. The partial run status (1 error, 120 files analyzed) may indicate a missing dependency — run npm install if ESLint issues are expected.

markdownlint runs with the project's own .markdownlint.json. The 17 findings (MD007 list indentation, MD032 list spacing, MD040 fenced code blocks) are governed by that config. Edit .markdownlint.json to adjust these rules.
